revision.text:
{{Expand Japanese|八剣宮}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hakkengū | native_name = 八剣宮 | image = Hakkenguu.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hakkengū | coordinates = {{coord|35.123498|136.908269}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|lt=Kusanagi|az|Kusanaqi (qılınc)|ca|Kusanagi|de|Kusanagi|en|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|es|Kusanagi|et|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|fa|کوساناگی|fr|Kusanagi|hu|Kuszanagi|id|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|it|Ama no Murakumo|ja|天叢雲剣|ko|쿠사나기의 검|pl|Miecz-Trawosiecz|pt|Kusanagi (espada)|ro|Kusanagi|ru|Кусанаги-но цуруги|sq|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|tr|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|uk|Кусанагі-но цуругі|vi|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|zh|天叢雲劍|qid=Q14944|1=Kusanagi no Tsurugi}} | established = {'time': '+0708-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} }} {{nihongo|'''Hakken-gū'''|八剣宮}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q9610964|ltq=Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Subshrine|lt=Shikinai Subshrine|qid=Q135100459|1=Shikinai Subshrine}} * {{ill|Q135009899|ltq=Q135009899|qid=Q135009899|1=Betsu-gū}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province|ja|尾張国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} ** P155: Province|lt=Owari Province|ar|أواري|ca|Província d'Owari|ckb|پارێزگای ئۆوواری|cs|Provincie Owari|de|Provinz Owari|en|Owari Province|es|Provincia de Owari|eu|Owari probintzia|fa|ولایت اوواری|fi|Owarin Owari|ru|Овари (провинция)|simple|Owari Province|sk|Owari (provincia)|sr|Провинција Овари|tr|Owari (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Оварі|vi|Tỉnh Owari|wuu|尾张国|zh_min_nan|Owari-kok|zh_yue|尾張國|zh|尾張國|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} * {{ill|Q552687|ltq=Q552687|qid=Q552687|1=Aichi District}} == coordinate location (P625) == * 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == architectural style (P149) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ヤツルキノ == location (P276) == * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|lt=Atsuta Jingū|ar|أتسوتا جينغو|az|Atsuta məbədi|cs|Svatyně Acuta|de|Atsuta-jingū|en|Atsuta Shrine|eu|Atsuta santutegia|fa|معبد آتسوتا|fr|Atsuta-jingū|hu|Acutai nagyszentély|id|Kuil Atsuta|it|Santuario di Atsuta|ja|熱田神宮|ko|아쓰타 신궁|la|Atsuta Templum|or|ଆତ୍ସୁତା ଶାଳ|pl|Atsuta-jingū|pt|Santuário de Atsuta|simple|Atsuta Shrine|th|ศาลเจ้าอัตสึตะ|tr|Atsuta Tapınağı|uk|Святилище Ацута|zh|熱田神宮|qid=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} == Commons category (P373) == * Hakken-gū == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県名古屋市熱田区神宮一丁目1-1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hakken-gū}} [[EN:Hakkengū]] [[JA:八剣宮]] {{wikidata link|Q55533377}} == Japanese content == {{複数の問題|出典の明記=June 2018|特筆性=June 2018}} {{表記揺れ案内|表記1=八劔宮}} {{神社 |名称 = Hakkengu |画像 = [[File:Hakkenguu.jpg|250px|Hakkengu, auxiliary shrine of Atsuta Jingu]] |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Atsuta Ward|Q1355625|qid=Q1355625|1=Atsuta-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Jingu (Nagoya)|Q30934394|qid=Q30934394|lt=Jingu|1=Jingū}}1-1-1 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 7 | 緯度秒 = 38.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 54 | 経度秒 = 31.2 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-23 |祭神 = Atsuta Omikami |神体 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Murakumo no Tsurugi|Q14944|qid=Q14944|lt=Kusanagi sacred sword (Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi)|1=Kusanagi no Tsurugi}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|qid=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}
Shikinaisha
Precinct auxiliary shrine of {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingu|Q482065|qid=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}}
Auxiliary shrine (bekku) of Atsuta Jingu |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=708|Q23511|qid=Q23511|1=708}} ({{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 1) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = Hakken Shrine
Hakken Myojin
Hakken Daimyojin
Shimo-no-Miya |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Nagoya }} '''Hakkengu''' (はっけんぐう) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Atsuta Ward|Q1355625|qid=Q1355625|1=Atsuta-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is an auxiliary shrine (bekku) of {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingu|Q482065|qid=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} and also a precinct auxiliary shrine. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' it is listed as "Hakken Shrine" in Aichi District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, and is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. === Enshrined deity === === Principal deity === * '''Atsuta Omikami''' ** Identified with '''{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}''', whose {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|qid=Q327532|1=Shintai}} is the '''Kusanagi sacred sword''' (also called Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi / Ame-no-Murakumo-no-Tsurugi), one of the {{ill|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|Q327465|qid=Q327465|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}}"Kusanagi sacred sword" is the official name used at Atsuta Jingu.. === Deities enshrined together === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} * {{ill|lt=Miyasu-hime|Q11455216|qid=Q11455216|lt=Miyasu-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Miyazu-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Taketane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691|qid=Q11486691|1=Takeinadane}} === History === It is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=708|Q23511|qid=Q23511|1=708}} ({{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|qid=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 1), when {{ill|lt=Empress Genmei|Q233224|qid=Q233224|1=Empress Genmei}} had a new sacred sword cast and dedicated following the {{ill|lt=Kusanagi sword theft incident|Q861812|qid=Q861812|1=theft of Kusanagi no Tsurugi}}. In the mid-Heian ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' it appears as "Hakken Shrine" in Aichi District, Owari Province. Concerning divine rank, the {{ill|lt=Jingi-cho of Owari Province|Q11041830|qid=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}} in the {{ill|lt=Joji|Q1146153|qid=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} manuscript has "Senior First Rank, Hakken Myojin," and the {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|qid=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} manuscript has "Senior First Rank Upper, Haken Daimyojin." In the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamakura period|1=UNKNOWN}}, it seems to have stood southeast of the Chinno-mon gate of Atsuta Jingu; a record in the ''Owari Chikatsugu Moshijo-an Dankan'' (compiled around the Bunryaku era) notes a {{ill|lt=fire|Q168983|qid=Q168983|1=Conflagration}} together with Hiwari-no-miya. In the ''{{ill|lt=Azuma Kagami|Q1042324|qid=Q1042324|1=Azuma Kagami}}'' entry for {{ill|lt=1180|Q19702|qid=Q19702|1=1180}} ({{ill|lt=Jisho|Q1194671|qid=Q1194671|1=Jishō}} 4), it appears as "Atsuta Hakken." In the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku era|1=Sengoku period}}, it was revered by the {{ill|lt=samurai class|Q2927809|qid=Q2927809|1=Buke}}, and figures such as {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} and {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|qid=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} built shrine buildings. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Hakken Myojin" or "Hakken Daimyojin" and stood slightly east of its current location. Records include reconstruction of the main hall by {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi|Q313745|qid=Q313745|1=Tokugawa Tsunayoshi}} in {{ill|lt=1686|Q7664|qid=Q7664|1=1686}} ({{ill|lt=Jokyo|Q1146221|qid=Q1146221|1=Jōkyō}} 3). In {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|qid=Q7806|1=1880}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 13), it was renamed the auxiliary shrine "Hakkengu." During shrine rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1893|Q7840|qid=Q7840|1=1893}} (Meiji 26), it moved to the current site. Its architectural style, annual festivals, and rites are all conducted in accordance with the main sanctuary of Atsuta Jingu. [[File:Atsuta Hongu oyobi Setsu-Matsu-sha no Zu.png|thumb|right|250px|Layout of Atsuta Jingu auxiliary shrines in the late Edo period]] === Location === * {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Atsuta Ward|Q1355625|qid=Q1355625|1=Atsuta-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Jingu (Nagoya)|Q30934394|qid=Q30934394|lt=Jingu|1=Jingū}}1-1-1, within the grounds of Atsuta Jingu. === Access === All are within walking distance. === Rail === * Nearest to the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=main gate|1=UNKNOWN}} (south gate): {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|Q1144874|qid=Q1144874|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway Meijo Line|Q906775|qid=Q906775|lt=Meijo Line|1=Meijō Line}} {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingu Temmacho Station|Q970033|qid=Q970033|1=Atsuta Jingu Temma-cho Station}}, Exit 1. * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|qid=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}} {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|qid=Q1137838|lt=Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Tokoname Line|Q6810420|qid=Q6810420|lt=Tokoname Line|1=Meitetsu Tokoname Line}} {{ill|lt=Jingumae Station|Q6202481|qid=Q6202481|1=Jingū-mae Station}}. * Nagoya Municipal Subway Meijo Line {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingu Nishi Station|Q903638|qid=Q903638|1=Atsuta Jingu Nishi Station}}. * {{ill|lt=JR Tokai|Q513679|qid=Q513679|lt=JR|1=Central Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Tokaido Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|qid=Q11235139|lt=Tokaido Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Line (JR Central)}} {{ill|lt=Atsuta Station|Q871814|qid=Q871814|1=Atsuta Station}}. === Bus === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Bus|Q11414982|qid=Q11414982|1=Nagoya Municipal Bus}} - get off at "Jingu Higashimon," "Atsuta Temmacho," "Atsuta Eki Nishi," "Atsuta Kuyakusho," or "Meitetsu Jingumae" stops; all are a short walk. === By car === * Parking available. During the first three days of New Year (January 1-3), parking is for related personnel only. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Miya-juku|Q3033374|qid=Q3033374|1=Miya-juku}} - 41st {{ill|lt=post station|Q1209783|qid=Q1209783|1=Shukuba}} on the {{ill|lt=Fifty-three Stations of the Tokaido|Q3134262|qid=Q3134262|1=53 Stations of the Tōkaidō}}; the temple-town of Atsuta Jingu. * {{ill|lt=Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)|Q11580543|qid=Q11580543|lt=Shiratori Kofun|1=Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)}} - said to be the mausoleum of Yamato Takeru. * {{ill|lt=Danpuzan Kofun|Q5221001|qid=Q5221001|1=Danpusan Kofun}} - largest {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} in the Tokai region; said to be the tomb of Miyasu-hime. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Matsuga Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} - auxiliary shrine of Atsuta Jingu, along {{ill|lt=National Route 1|Q1191191|qid=Q1191191|1=Japan National Route 1}} near Atsuta Jingu's main gate. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingu|Q482065|qid=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Hakken Shrine|Q11390593|qid=Q11390593|1=Yatsurugi Shrine (disambiguation)}} === External links === {{Commonscat|hakkengu}} * [https://www.atsutajingu.or.jp/jingu/about/keidai/02.html Auxiliary Shrine Hakkengu] - Atsuta Jingu official site {{熱田神宮45社}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hakkengū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hakkengū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hakkengū]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:熱田神宮]] [[Category:Three sacred treasures of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Atsuta-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:熱田区の歴史]] [[Category:Shinmei-zukuri]] [[Category:700年代設立]]
